name: Garlic-Ginger Chicken Wings
instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with aluminum foil and lightly coat it with cooking spray to prevent sticking.

2. In a large bowl, season the chicken wings generously with hot sauce, salt, and black pepper. Drizzle with vegetable oil and toss thoroughly to ensure all wings are evenly coated.

3. Place the seasoned wings and flour in a large food-safe plastic bag. Seal the bag and shake vigorously until every wing is completely coated with flour.

4. Arrange the floured wings on the prepared baking sheet in a single layer, making sure no wings touch one another to ensure even crisping. Lightly spray the tops of the wings with cooking spray.

5. Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes. Carefully flip each wing over and continue baking until the wings are golden, crispy, and no longer pink in the center, about 30 minutes more.

6. While the wings bake, prepare the glaze. In a small saucepan, whisk together the rice wine vinegar, brown sugar, minced garlic, grated ginger, chili paste, and soy sauce. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil over medium-high heat, then immediately remove from heat and set aside.

7. Using tongs, transfer the hot baked wings to a large bowl. Pour the glaze over the wings and toss until evenly coated. Transfer to a serving platter and let sit for about 5 minutes to allow the glaze to soak into the wings before serving.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F and prep a foil-lined baking sheet.
2. Season wings with hot sauce, salt, pepper, and oil, then shake in flour until coated.
3. Bake 30 minutes, flip, then bake 30 more until crispy and golden.
4. Boil glaze ingredients, toss hot wings in glaze, let sit 5 minutes, and enjoy!
